items.name: Vesting
docs: Vesting schedule for a claim.
First balance is the total amount that should be held for vesting.
Second balance is how much should be unlocked per block.
The block number is when the vesting should start.

items.name: Signing
docs: The statement kind that must be signed, if any.

items.name: Preclaims
docs: Pre-claimed Ethereum accounts, by the Account ID that they are claimed to.

constants.name: batched_calls_limit
docs: The limit on the number of batched calls.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: The overarching call type.

associatedTypes.name: PalletsOrigin
docs: The caller origin, overarching type of all pallets origins.

items.name: IdentityOf
docs: Information that is pertinent to identify the entity behind an account. First item is the
registration, second is the account's primary username.
TWOX-NOTE: OK ― `AccountId` is a secure hash.

items.name: UsernameOf
docs: Identifies the primary username of an account.

items.name: SuperOf
docs: The super-identity of an alternative "sub" identity together with its name, within that
context. If the account is not some other account's sub-identity, then just `None`.

items.name: SubsOf
docs: Alternative "sub" identities of this account.
The first item is the deposit, the second is a vector of the accounts.
TWOX-NOTE: OK ― `AccountId` is a secure hash.

items.name: Registrars
docs: The set of registrars. Not expected to get very big as can only be added through a
special origin (likely a council motion).
The index into this can be cast to `RegistrarIndex` to get a valid value.

items.name: AuthorityOf
docs: A map of the accounts who are authorized to grant usernames.

items.name: UsernameInfoOf
docs: Reverse lookup from `username` to the `AccountId` that has registered it and the provider of
the username. The `owner` value should be a key in the `UsernameOf` map, but it may not if
the user has cleared their username or it has been removed.
Multiple usernames may map to the same `AccountId`, but `UsernameOf` will only map to one
primary username.

items.name: PendingUsernames
docs: Usernames that an authority has granted, but that the account controller has not confirmed
that they want it. Used primarily in cases where the `AccountId` cannot provide a signature
because they are a pure proxy, multisig, etc. In order to confirm it, they should call
[accept_username](`Call::accept_username`).
First tuple item is the account and second is the acceptance deadline.

items.name: UnbindingUsernames
docs: Usernames for which the authority that granted them has started the removal process by
unbinding them. Each unbinding username maps to its grace period expiry, which is the first
block in which the username could be deleted through a
[remove_username](`Call::remove_username`) call.

constants.name: BasicDeposit
docs: The amount held on deposit for a registered identity.

constants.name: ByteDeposit
docs: The amount held on deposit per encoded byte for a registered identity.

constants.name: UsernameDeposit
docs: The amount held on deposit per registered username. This value should change only in
runtime upgrades with proper migration of existing deposits.

constants.name: SubAccountDeposit
docs: The amount held on deposit for a registered subaccount. This should account for the fact
that one storage item's value will increase by the size of an account ID, and there will
be another trie item whose value is the size of an account ID plus 32 bytes.

constants.name: MaxSubAccounts
docs: The maximum number of sub-accounts allowed per identified account.

constants.name: MaxRegistrars
docs: Maximum number of registrars allowed in the system. Needed to bound the complexity
of, e.g., updating judgements.

constants.name: PendingUsernameExpiration
docs: The number of blocks within which a username grant must be accepted.

constants.name: UsernameGracePeriod
docs: The number of blocks that must pass to enable the permanent deletion of a username by
its respective authority.

constants.name: MaxSuffixLength
docs: The maximum length of a suffix.

constants.name: MaxUsernameLength
docs: The maximum length of a username, including its suffix and any system-added delimiters.

associatedTypes.name: OffchainSignature
docs: Signature type for pre-authorizing usernames off-chain.
Can verify whether an `Self::SigningPublicKey` created a signature.

items.name: Parameters
docs: The max number of members for the society at one time.

items.name: Pot
docs: Amount of our account balance that is specifically for the next round's bid(s).

items.name: Founder
docs: The first member.

items.name: Head
docs: The most primary from the most recently approved rank 0 members in the society.

items.name: Rules
docs: A hash of the rules of this society concerning membership. Can only be set once and
only by the founder.

items.name: Members
docs: The current members and their rank. Doesn't include `SuspendedMembers`.

items.name: Payouts
docs: Information regarding rank-0 payouts, past and future.

items.name: MemberCount
docs: The number of items in `Members` currently. (Doesn't include `SuspendedMembers`.)

items.name: MemberByIndex
docs: The current items in `Members` keyed by their unique index. Keys are densely populated
`0..MemberCount` (does not include `MemberCount`).

items.name: SuspendedMembers
docs: The set of suspended members, with their old membership record.

items.name: RoundCount
docs: The number of rounds which have passed.

items.name: Bids
docs: The current bids, stored ordered by the value of the bid.

items.name: Skeptic
docs: The current skeptic.

items.name: Votes
docs: Double map from Candidate -> Voter -> (Maybe) Vote.

items.name: VoteClearCursor
docs: Clear-cursor for Vote, map from Candidate -> (Maybe) Cursor.

items.name: NextHead
docs: At the end of the claim period, this contains the most recently approved members (along with
their bid and round ID) who is from the most recent round with the lowest bid. They will
become the new `Head`.

items.name: ChallengeRoundCount
docs: The number of challenge rounds there have been. Used to identify stale DefenderVotes.

items.name: Defending
docs: The defending member currently being challenged, along with a running tally of votes.

items.name: DefenderVotes
docs: Votes for the defender, keyed by challenge round.

constants.name: PalletId
docs: The societies's pallet id

constants.name: GraceStrikes
docs: The maximum number of strikes before a member gets funds slashed.

constants.name: PeriodSpend
docs: The amount of incentive paid within each period. Doesn't include VoterTip.

constants.name: VotingPeriod
docs: The number of blocks on which new candidates should be voted on. Together with
`ClaimPeriod`, this sums to the number of blocks between candidate intake periods.

constants.name: ClaimPeriod
docs: The number of blocks on which new candidates can claim their membership and be the
named head.

constants.name: MaxLockDuration
docs: The maximum duration of the payout lock.

constants.name: ChallengePeriod
docs: The number of blocks between membership challenges.

constants.name: MaxPayouts
docs: The maximum number of payouts a member may have waiting unclaimed.

constants.name: MaxBids
docs: The maximum number of bids at once.

items.name: Recoverable
docs: The set of recoverable accounts and their recovery configuration.

items.name: ActiveRecoveries
docs: Active recovery attempts.
First account is the account to be recovered, and the second account
is the user trying to recover the account.

items.name: Proxy
docs: The list of allowed proxy accounts.
Map from the user who can access it to the recovered account.

constants.name: ConfigDepositBase
docs: The base amount of currency needed to reserve for creating a recovery configuration.
This is held for an additional storage item whose value size is
`2 + sizeof(BlockNumber, Balance)` bytes.

constants.name: FriendDepositFactor
docs: The amount of currency needed per additional user when creating a recovery
configuration.
This is held for adding `sizeof(AccountId)` bytes more into a pre-existing storage
value.

constants.name: MaxFriends
docs: The maximum amount of friends allowed in a recovery configuration.
NOTE: The threshold programmed in this Pallet uses u16, so it does
not really make sense to have a limit here greater than u16::MAX.
But also, that is a lot more than you should probably set this value
to anyway...

constants.name: RecoveryDeposit
docs: The base amount of currency needed to reserve for starting a recovery.
This is primarily held for deterring malicious recovery attempts, and should
have a value large enough that a bad actor would choose not to place this
deposit. It also acts to fund additional storage item whose value size is
`sizeof(BlockNumber, Balance + T * AccountId)` bytes. Where T is a configurable
threshold.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: The overarching call type.

items.name: Vesting
docs: Information regarding the vesting of a given account.

items.name: StorageVersion
docs: Storage version of the pallet.
New networks start with latest version, as determined by the genesis build.

constants.name: MinVestedTransfer
docs: The minimum amount transferred to call `vested_transfer`.

items.name: IncompleteSince
docs: Block number at which the agenda began incomplete execution.

items.name: Agenda
docs: Items to be executed, indexed by the block number that they should be executed on.

items.name: Retries
docs: Retry configurations for items to be executed, indexed by task address.

items.name: Lookup
docs: Lookup from a name to the block number and index of the task.
For v3 -> v4 the previously unbounded identities are Blake2-256 hashed to form the v4
identities.

constants.name: MaximumWeight
docs: The maximum weight that may be scheduled per block for any dispatchables.

constants.name: MaxScheduledPerBlock
docs: The maximum number of scheduled calls in the queue for a single block.
NOTE:
+ Dependent pallets' benchmarks might require a higher limit for the setting. Set a
higher limit under `runtime-benchmarks` feature.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: The aggregated call type.

items.name: Proxies
docs: The set of account proxies. Maps the account which has delegated to the accounts
which are being delegated to, together with the amount held on deposit.

items.name: Announcements
docs: The announcements made by the proxy (key).

constants.name: ProxyDepositBase
docs: The base amount of currency needed to reserve for creating a proxy.
This is held for an additional storage item whose value size is
`sizeof(Balance)` bytes and whose key size is `sizeof(AccountId)` bytes.

constants.name: ProxyDepositFactor
docs: The amount of currency needed per proxy added.
This is held for adding 32 bytes plus an instance of `ProxyType` more into a
pre-existing storage value. Thus, when configuring `ProxyDepositFactor` one should take
into account `32 + proxy_type.encode().len()` bytes of data.

constants.name: MaxProxies
docs: The maximum amount of proxies allowed for a single account.

constants.name: MaxPending
docs: The maximum amount of time-delayed announcements that are allowed to be pending.

constants.name: AnnouncementDepositBase
docs: The base amount of currency needed to reserve for creating an announcement.
This is held when a new storage item holding a `Balance` is created (typically 16
bytes).

constants.name: AnnouncementDepositFactor
docs: The amount of currency needed per announcement made.
This is held for adding an `AccountId`, `Hash` and `BlockNumber` (typically 68 bytes)
into a pre-existing storage value.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: The overarching call type.

associatedTypes.name: ProxyType
docs: A kind of proxy; specified with the proxy and passed in to the `IsProxyable` filter.
The instance filter determines whether a given call may be proxied under this type.
IMPORTANT: `Default` must be provided and MUST BE the the *most permissive* value.

viewFunctions.name: check_permissions
docs: Check if a `RuntimeCall` is allowed for a given `ProxyType`.

viewFunctions.name: is_superset
docs: Check if one `ProxyType` is a subset of another `ProxyType`.

items.name: Multisigs
docs: The set of open multisig operations.

constants.name: DepositBase
docs: The base amount of currency needed to reserve for creating a multisig execution or to
store a dispatch call for later.
This is held for an additional storage item whose value size is
`4 + sizeof((BlockNumber, Balance, AccountId))` bytes and whose key size is
`32 + sizeof(AccountId)` bytes.

constants.name: DepositFactor
docs: The amount of currency needed per unit threshold when creating a multisig execution.
This is held for adding 32 bytes more into a pre-existing storage value.

constants.name: MaxSignatories
docs: The maximum amount of signatories allowed in the multisig.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: The overarching call type.

items.name: StatusFor
docs: The request status of a given hash.

items.name: RequestStatusFor
docs: The request status of a given hash.

items.name: ConversionRateToNative
docs: Maps an asset to its fixed point representation in the native balance.
E.g. `native_amount = asset_amount * ConversionRateToNative::<T>::get(asset_kind)`

associatedTypes.name: AssetKind
docs: The type for asset kinds for which the conversion rate to native balance is set.

items.name: BountyCount
docs: Number of bounty proposals that have been made.

items.name: Bounties
docs: Bounties that have been made.

items.name: BountyDescriptions
docs: The description of each bounty.

items.name: BountyApprovals
docs: Bounty indices that have been approved but not yet funded.

constants.name: BountyDepositBase
docs: The amount held on deposit for placing a bounty proposal.

constants.name: BountyDepositPayoutDelay
docs: The delay period for which a bounty beneficiary need to wait before claim the payout.

constants.name: BountyUpdatePeriod
docs: The time limit for a curator to act before a bounty expires.
The period that starts when a curator is approved, during which they must execute or
update the bounty via `extend_bounty_expiry`. If missed, the bounty expires, and the
curator may be slashed. If `BlockNumberFor::MAX`, bounties stay active indefinitely,
removing the need for `extend_bounty_expiry`.

constants.name: CuratorDepositMultiplier
docs: The curator deposit is calculated as a percentage of the curator fee.
This deposit has optional upper and lower bounds with `CuratorDepositMax` and
`CuratorDepositMin`.

constants.name: CuratorDepositMax
docs: Maximum amount of funds that should be placed in a deposit for making a proposal.

constants.name: CuratorDepositMin
docs: Minimum amount of funds that should be placed in a deposit for making a proposal.

constants.name: BountyValueMinimum
docs: Minimum value for a bounty.

constants.name: DataDepositPerByte
docs: The amount held on deposit per byte within the tip report reason or bounty description.

constants.name: MaximumReasonLength
docs: Maximum acceptable reason length.
Benchmarks depend on this value, be sure to update weights file when changing this value

items.name: ChildBountyCount
docs: DEPRECATED: Replaced with `ParentTotalChildBounties` storage item keeping dedicated counts
for each parent bounty. Number of total child bounties. Will be removed in May 2025.

items.name: ParentChildBounties
docs: Number of active child bounties per parent bounty.
Map of parent bounty index to number of child bounties.

items.name: ParentTotalChildBounties
docs: Number of total child bounties per parent bounty, including completed bounties.

items.name: ChildBounties
docs: Child bounties that have been added.

items.name: ChildBountyDescriptionsV1
docs: The description of each child-bounty. Indexed by `(parent_id, child_id)`.
This item replaces the `ChildBountyDescriptions` storage item from the V0 storage version.

items.name: V0ToV1ChildBountyIds
docs: The mapping of the child bounty ids from storage version `V0` to the new `V1` version.
The `V0` ids based on total child bounty count [`ChildBountyCount`]`. The `V1` version ids
based on the child bounty count per parent bounty [`ParentTotalChildBounties`].
The item intended solely for client convenience and not used in the pallet's core logic.

items.name: ChildrenCuratorFees
docs: The cumulative child-bounty curator fee for each parent bounty.

constants.name: MaxActiveChildBountyCount
docs: Maximum number of child bounties that can be added to a parent bounty.

constants.name: ChildBountyValueMinimum
docs: Minimum value for a child-bounty.

items.name: QueueTotals
docs: The totals of items and balances within each queue. Saves a lot of storage reads in the
case of sparsely packed queues.
The vector is indexed by duration in `Period`s, offset by one, so information on the queue
whose duration is one `Period` would be storage `0`.

items.name: Queues
docs: The queues of bids. Indexed by duration (in `Period`s).

items.name: Summary
docs: Summary information over the general state.

items.name: Receipts
docs: The currently outstanding receipts, indexed according to the order of creation.

constants.name: PalletId
docs: The treasury's pallet id, used for deriving its sovereign account ID.

constants.name: QueueCount
docs: Number of duration queues in total. This sets the maximum duration supported, which is
this value multiplied by `Period`.

constants.name: MaxQueueLen
docs: Maximum number of items that may be in each duration queue.
Must be larger than zero.

constants.name: FifoQueueLen
docs: Portion of the queue which is free from ordering and just a FIFO.
Must be no greater than `MaxQueueLen`.

constants.name: BasePeriod
docs: The base period for the duration queues. This is the common multiple across all
supported freezing durations that can be bid upon.

constants.name: MinBid
docs: The minimum amount of funds that may be placed in a bid. Note that this
does not actually limit the amount which may be represented in a receipt since bids may
be split up by the system.
It should be at least big enough to ensure that there is no possible storage spam attack
or queue-filling attack.

constants.name: MinReceipt
docs: The minimum amount of funds which may intentionally be left remaining under a single
receipt.

constants.name: IntakePeriod
docs: The number of blocks between consecutive attempts to dequeue bids and create receipts.
A larger value results in fewer storage hits each block, but a slower period to get to
the target.

constants.name: MaxIntakeWeight
docs: The maximum amount of bids that can consolidated into receipts in a single intake. A
larger value here means less of the block available for transactions should there be a
glut of bids.

constants.name: ThawThrottle
docs: The maximum proportion which may be thawed and the period over which it is reset.

items.name: TotalIssuance
docs: The total units issued in the system.

items.name: InactiveIssuance
docs: The total units of outstanding deactivated balance in the system.

items.name: Account
docs: The Balances pallet example of storing the balance of an account.
# Example
```nocompile
impl pallet_balances::Config for Runtime {
type AccountStore = StorageMapShim<Self::Account<Runtime>, frame_system::Provider<Runtime>, AccountId, Self::AccountData<Balance>>
You can also store the balance of an account in the `System` pallet.
# Example
```nocompile
impl pallet_balances::Config for Runtime {
type AccountStore = System
But this comes with tradeoffs, storing account balances in the system pallet stores
`frame_system` data alongside the account data contrary to storing account balances in the
`Balances` pallet, which uses a `StorageMap` to store balances data only.
NOTE: This is only used in the case that this pallet is used to store balances.

items.name: Locks
docs: Any liquidity locks on some account balances.
NOTE: Should only be accessed when setting, changing and freeing a lock.
Use of locks is deprecated in favour of freezes. See `https://github.com/paritytech/substrate/pull/12951/`

items.name: Reserves
docs: Named reserves on some account balances.
Use of reserves is deprecated in favour of holds. See `https://github.com/paritytech/substrate/pull/12951/`

items.name: Holds
docs: Holds on account balances.

items.name: Freezes
docs: Freeze locks on account balances.

constants.name: ExistentialDeposit
docs: The minimum amount required to keep an account open. MUST BE GREATER THAN ZERO!
If you *really* need it to be zero, you can enable the feature `insecure_zero_ed` for
this pallet. However, you do so at your own risk: this will open up a major DoS vector.
In case you have multiple sources of provider references, you may also get unexpected
behaviour if you set this to zero.
Bottom line: Do yourself a favour and make it at least one!

constants.name: MaxLocks
docs: The maximum number of locks that should exist on an account.
Not strictly enforced, but used for weight estimation.
Use of locks is deprecated in favour of freezes. See `https://github.com/paritytech/substrate/pull/12951/`

constants.name: MaxReserves
docs: The maximum number of named reserves that can exist on an account.
Use of reserves is deprecated in favour of holds. See `https://github.com/paritytech/substrate/pull/12951/`

constants.name: MaxFreezes
docs: The maximum number of individual freeze locks that can exist on an account at any time.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeHoldReason
docs: The overarching hold reason.

associatedTypes.name: Balance
docs: The balance of an account.

associatedTypes.name: ReserveIdentifier
docs: The ID type for reserves.
Use of reserves is deprecated in favour of holds. See `https://github.com/paritytech/substrate/pull/12951/`

associatedTypes.name: FreezeIdentifier
docs: The ID type for freezes.

pallets.name: ParachainsOrigin
docs: There is no way to register an origin type in `construct_runtime` without a pallet the origin
belongs to.
This module fulfills only the single purpose of housing the `Origin` in `construct_runtime`.

items.name: ActiveConfig
docs: The active configuration for the current session.

items.name: PendingConfigs
docs: Pending configuration changes.
This is a list of configuration changes, each with a session index at which it should
be applied.
The list is sorted ascending by session index. Also, this list can only contain at most
2 items: for the next session and for the `scheduled_session`.

items.name: BypassConsistencyCheck
docs: If this is set, then the configuration setters will bypass the consistency checks. This
is meant to be used only as the last resort.

items.name: CurrentSessionIndex
docs: The current session index.

items.name: ActiveValidatorIndices
docs: All the validators actively participating in parachain consensus.
Indices are into the broader validator set.

items.name: ActiveValidatorKeys
docs: The parachain attestation keys of the validators actively participating in parachain
consensus. This should be the same length as `ActiveValidatorIndices`.

items.name: AllowedRelayParents
docs: All allowed relay-parents.

items.name: V1
docs: Candidates pending availability by `ParaId`. They form a chain starting from the latest
included head of the para.
Use a different prefix post-migration to v1, since the v0 `PendingAvailability` storage
would otherwise have the exact same prefix which could cause undefined behaviour when doing
the migration.

items.name: Included
docs: Whether the paras inherent was included within this block.
The `Option<()>` is effectively a `bool`, but it never hits storage in the `None` variant
due to the guarantees of FRAME's storage APIs.
If this is `None` at the end of the block, we panic and render the block invalid.

items.name: OnChainVotes
docs: Scraped on chain data for extracting resolved disputes as well as backing votes.

items.name: ValidatorGroups
docs: All the validator groups. One for each core. Indices are into `ActiveValidators` - not the
broader set of Polkadot validators, but instead just the subset used for parachains during
this session.
Bound: The number of cores is the sum of the numbers of parachains and parathread
multiplexers. Reasonably, 100-1000. The dominant factor is the number of validators: safe
upper bound at 10k.

items.name: SessionStartBlock
docs: The block number where the session start occurred. Used to track how many group rotations
have occurred.
Note that in the context of parachains modules the session change is signaled during
the block and enacted at the end of the block (at the finalization stage, to be exact).
Thus for all intents and purposes the effect of the session change is observed at the
block following the session change, block number of which we save in this storage value.

items.name: ClaimQueue
docs: One entry for each availability core. The `VecDeque` represents the assignments to be
scheduled on that core.

items.name: PvfActiveVoteMap
docs: All currently active PVF pre-checking votes.
Invariant:
- There are no PVF pre-checking votes that exists in list but not in the set and vice versa.

items.name: PvfActiveVoteList
docs: The list of all currently active PVF votes. Auxiliary to `PvfActiveVoteMap`.

items.name: Parachains
docs: All lease holding parachains. Ordered ascending by `ParaId`. On demand parachains are not
included.
Consider using the [`ParachainsCache`] type of modifying.

items.name: ParaLifecycles
docs: The current lifecycle of a all known Para IDs.

items.name: Heads
docs: The head-data of every registered para.

items.name: MostRecentContext
docs: The context (relay-chain block number) of the most recent parachain head.

items.name: CurrentCodeHash
docs: The validation code hash of every live para.
Corresponding code can be retrieved with [`CodeByHash`].

items.name: PastCodeHash
docs: Actual past code hash, indicated by the para id as well as the block number at which it
became outdated.
Corresponding code can be retrieved with [`CodeByHash`].

items.name: PastCodeMeta
docs: Past code of parachains. The parachains themselves may not be registered anymore,
but we also keep their code on-chain for the same amount of time as outdated code
to keep it available for approval checkers.

items.name: PastCodePruning
docs: Which paras have past code that needs pruning and the relay-chain block at which the code
was replaced. Note that this is the actual height of the included block, not the expected
height at which the code upgrade would be applied, although they may be equal.
This is to ensure the entire acceptance period is covered, not an offset acceptance period
starting from the time at which the parachain perceives a code upgrade as having occurred.
Multiple entries for a single para are permitted. Ordered ascending by block number.

items.name: FutureCodeUpgrades
docs: The block number at which the planned code change is expected for a parachain.
The change will be applied after the first parablock for this ID included which executes
in the context of a relay chain block with a number >= `expected_at`.

items.name: FutureCodeUpgradesAt
docs: The list of upcoming future code upgrades.
Each item is a pair of the parachain and the expected block at which the upgrade should be
applied. The upgrade will be applied at the given relay chain block. In contrast to
[`FutureCodeUpgrades`] this code upgrade will be applied regardless the parachain making any
progress or not.
Ordered ascending by block number.

items.name: FutureCodeHash
docs: The actual future code hash of a para.
Corresponding code can be retrieved with [`CodeByHash`].

items.name: UpgradeGoAheadSignal
docs: This is used by the relay-chain to communicate to a parachain a go-ahead with in the upgrade
procedure.
This value is absent when there are no upgrades scheduled or during the time the relay chain
performs the checks. It is set at the first relay-chain block when the corresponding
parachain can switch its upgrade function. As soon as the parachain's block is included, the
value gets reset to `None`.
NOTE that this field is used by parachains via merkle storage proofs, therefore changing
the format will require migration of parachains.

items.name: UpgradeRestrictionSignal
docs: This is used by the relay-chain to communicate that there are restrictions for performing
an upgrade for this parachain.
This may be a because the parachain waits for the upgrade cooldown to expire. Another
potential use case is when we want to perform some maintenance (such as storage migration)
we could restrict upgrades to make the process simpler.
NOTE that this field is used by parachains via merkle storage proofs, therefore changing
the format will require migration of parachains.

items.name: UpgradeCooldowns
docs: The list of parachains that are awaiting for their upgrade restriction to cooldown.
Ordered ascending by block number.

items.name: UpcomingUpgrades
docs: The list of upcoming code upgrades.
Each item is a pair of which para performs a code upgrade and at which relay-chain block it
is expected at.
Ordered ascending by block number.

items.name: ActionsQueue
docs: The actions to perform during the start of a specific session index.

items.name: UpcomingParasGenesis
docs: Upcoming paras instantiation arguments.
NOTE that after PVF pre-checking is enabled the para genesis arg will have it's code set
to empty. Instead, the code will be saved into the storage right away via `CodeByHash`.

items.name: CodeByHashRefs
docs: The number of reference on the validation code in [`CodeByHash`] storage.

items.name: CodeByHash
docs: Validation code stored by its hash.
This storage is consistent with [`FutureCodeHash`], [`CurrentCodeHash`] and
[`PastCodeHash`].

viewFunctions.name: remove_upgrade_cooldown_cost
docs: Returns the cost for removing an upgrade cooldown for the given `para`.

items.name: HasInitialized
docs: Whether the parachains modules have been initialized within this block.
Semantically a `bool`, but this guarantees it should never hit the trie,
as this is cleared in `on_finalize` and Frame optimizes `None` values to be empty values.
As a `bool`, `set(false)` and `remove()` both lead to the next `get()` being false, but one
of them writes to the trie and one does not. This confusion makes `Option<()>` more suitable
for the semantics of this variable.

items.name: BufferedSessionChanges
docs: Buffered session changes.
Typically this will be empty or one element long. Apart from that this item never hits
the storage.
However this is a `Vec` regardless to handle various edge cases that may occur at runtime
upgrade boundaries or if governance intervenes.

items.name: DownwardMessageQueues
docs: The downward messages addressed for a certain para.

items.name: DownwardMessageQueueHeads
docs: A mapping that stores the downward message queue MQC head for each para.
Each link in this chain has a form:
`(prev_head, B, H(M))`, where
- `prev_head`: is the previous head hash or zero if none.
- `B`: is the relay-chain block number in which a message was appended.
- `H(M)`: is the hash of the message being appended.

items.name: DeliveryFeeFactor
docs: The factor to multiply the base delivery fee by.

items.name: HrmpOpenChannelRequests
docs: The set of pending HRMP open channel requests.
The set is accompanied by a list for iteration.
Invariant:
- There are no channels that exists in list but not in the set and vice versa.

items.name: HrmpOpenChannelRequestCount
docs: This mapping tracks how many open channel requests are initiated by a given sender para.
Invariant: `HrmpOpenChannelRequests` should contain the same number of items that has
`(X, _)` as the number of `HrmpOpenChannelRequestCount` for `X`.

items.name: HrmpAcceptedChannelRequestCount
docs: This mapping tracks how many open channel requests were accepted by a given recipient para.
Invariant: `HrmpOpenChannelRequests` should contain the same number of items `(_, X)` with
`confirmed` set to true, as the number of `HrmpAcceptedChannelRequestCount` for `X`.

items.name: HrmpCloseChannelRequests
docs: A set of pending HRMP close channel requests that are going to be closed during the session
change. Used for checking if a given channel is registered for closure.
The set is accompanied by a list for iteration.
Invariant:
- There are no channels that exists in list but not in the set and vice versa.

items.name: HrmpWatermarks
docs: The HRMP watermark associated with each para.
Invariant:
- each para `P` used here as a key should satisfy `Paras::is_valid_para(P)` within a
session.

items.name: HrmpChannels
docs: HRMP channel data associated with each para.
Invariant:
- each participant in the channel should satisfy `Paras::is_valid_para(P)` within a session.

items.name: HrmpIngressChannelsIndex
docs: Ingress/egress indexes allow to find all the senders and receivers given the opposite side.
I.e.
(a) ingress index allows to find all the senders for a given recipient.
(b) egress index allows to find all the recipients for a given sender.
Invariants:
- for each ingress index entry for `P` each item `I` in the index should present in
`HrmpChannels` as `(I, P)`.
- for each egress index entry for `P` each item `E` in the index should present in
`HrmpChannels` as `(P, E)`.
- there should be no other dangling channels in `HrmpChannels`.
- the vectors are sorted.

items.name: HrmpChannelContents
docs: Storage for the messages for each channel.
Invariant: cannot be non-empty if the corresponding channel in `HrmpChannels` is `None`.

items.name: HrmpChannelDigests
docs: Maintains a mapping that can be used to answer the question: What paras sent a message at
the given block number for a given receiver. Invariants:
- The inner `Vec<ParaId>` is never empty.
- The inner `Vec<ParaId>` cannot store two same `ParaId`.
- The outer vector is sorted ascending by block number and cannot store two items with the
same block number.

items.name: AssignmentKeysUnsafe
docs: Assignment keys for the current session.
Note that this API is private due to it being prone to 'off-by-one' at session boundaries.
When in doubt, use `Sessions` API instead.

items.name: EarliestStoredSession
docs: The earliest session for which previous session info is stored.

items.name: Sessions
docs: Session information in a rolling window.
Should have an entry in range `EarliestStoredSession..=CurrentSessionIndex`.
Does not have any entries before the session index in the first session change notification.

items.name: AccountKeys
docs: The validator account keys of the validators actively participating in parachain consensus.

items.name: SessionExecutorParams
docs: Executor parameter set for a given session index

items.name: LastPrunedSession
docs: The last pruned session, if any. All data stored by this module
references sessions.

items.name: Disputes
docs: All ongoing or concluded disputes for the last several sessions.

items.name: BackersOnDisputes
docs: Backing votes stored for each dispute.
This storage is used for slashing.

items.name: Included
docs: All included blocks on the chain, as well as the block number in this chain that
should be reverted back to if the candidate is disputed and determined to be invalid.

items.name: Frozen
docs: Whether the chain is frozen. Starts as `None`. When this is `Some`,
the chain will not accept any new parachain blocks for backing or inclusion,
and its value indicates the last valid block number in the chain.
It can only be set back to `None` by governance intervention.

items.name: UnappliedSlashes
docs: Validators pending dispute slashes.

items.name: ValidatorSetCounts
docs: `ValidatorSetCount` per session.

associatedTypes.name: KeyOwnerProof
docs: The proof of key ownership, used for validating slashing reports.
The proof must include the session index and validator count of the
session at which the offence occurred.

associatedTypes.name: KeyOwnerIdentification
docs: The identification of a key owner, used when reporting slashes.

items.name: BookStateFor
docs: The index of the first and last (non-empty) pages.

items.name: ServiceHead
docs: The origin at which we should begin servicing.

items.name: Pages
docs: The map of page indices to pages.

constants.name: HeapSize
docs: The size of the page; this implies the maximum message size which can be sent.
A good value depends on the expected message sizes, their weights, the weight that is
available for processing them and the maximal needed message size. The maximal message
size is slightly lower than this as defined by [`MaxMessageLenOf`].

constants.name: MaxStale
docs: The maximum number of stale pages (i.e. of overweight messages) allowed before culling
can happen. Once there are more stale pages than this, then historical pages may be
dropped, even if they contain unprocessed overweight messages.

constants.name: ServiceWeight
docs: The amount of weight (if any) which should be provided to the message queue for
servicing enqueued items `on_initialize`.
This may be legitimately `None` in the case that you will call
`ServiceQueues::service_queues` manually or set [`Self::IdleMaxServiceWeight`] to have
it run in `on_idle`.

constants.name: IdleMaxServiceWeight
docs: The maximum amount of weight (if any) to be used from remaining weight `on_idle` which
should be provided to the message queue for servicing enqueued items `on_idle`.
Useful for parachains to process messages at the same block they are received.
If `None`, it will not call `ServiceQueues::service_queues` in `on_idle`.

associatedTypes.name: Size
docs: Page/heap size type.

items.name: ParaIdAffinity
docs: Maps a `ParaId` to `CoreIndex` and keeps track of how many assignments the scheduler has in
it's lookahead. Keeping track of this affinity prevents parallel execution of the same
`ParaId` on two or more `CoreIndex`es.

items.name: QueueStatus
docs: Overall status of queue (both free + affinity entries)

items.name: FreeEntries
docs: Priority queue for all orders which don't yet (or not any more) have any core affinity.

items.name: AffinityEntries
docs: Queue entries that are currently bound to a particular core due to core affinity.

items.name: Revenue
docs: Keeps track of accumulated revenue from on demand order sales.

items.name: Credits
docs: Keeps track of credits owned by each account.

value.note: This will be removed in favor of using `place_order_with_credits`
This will be removed in favor of using `place_order_with_credits`

constants.name: TrafficDefaultValue
docs: The default value for the spot traffic multiplier.

constants.name: MaxHistoricalRevenue
docs: The maximum number of blocks some historical revenue
information stored for.

constants.name: PalletId
docs: Identifier for the internal revenue balance.

items.name: CoreSchedules
docs: Scheduled assignment sets.
Assignments as of the given block number. They will go into state once the block number is
reached (and replace whatever was in there before).

items.name: CoreDescriptors
docs: Assignments which are currently active.
They will be picked from `PendingAssignments` once we reach the scheduled block number in
`PendingAssignments`.

items.name: PendingSwap
docs: Pending swap operations.

items.name: Paras
docs: Amount held on deposit for each para and the original depositor.
The given account ID is responsible for registering the code and initial head data, but may
only do so if it isn't yet registered. (After that, it's up to governance to do so.)

items.name: NextFreeParaId
docs: The next free `ParaId`.

constants.name: ParaDeposit
docs: The deposit to be paid to run a on-demand parachain.
This should include the cost for storing the genesis head and validation code.

constants.name: DataDepositPerByte
docs: The deposit to be paid per byte stored on chain.

items.name: Leases
docs: Amounts held on deposit for each (possibly future) leased parachain.
The actual amount locked on its behalf by any account at any time is the maximum of the
second values of the items in this list whose first value is the account.
The first item in the list is the amount locked for the current Lease Period. Following
items are for the subsequent lease periods.
The default value (an empty list) implies that the parachain no longer exists (or never
existed) as far as this pallet is concerned.
If a parachain doesn't exist *yet* but is scheduled to exist in the future, then it
will be left-padded with one or more `None`s to denote the fact that nothing is held on
deposit for the non-existent chain currently, but is held at some point in the future.
It is illegal for a `None` value to trail in the list.

constants.name: LeasePeriod
docs: The number of blocks over which a single period lasts.

constants.name: LeaseOffset
docs: The number of blocks to offset each lease period by.

items.name: AuctionCounter
docs: Number of auctions started so far.

items.name: AuctionInfo
docs: Information relating to the current auction, if there is one.
The first item in the tuple is the lease period index that the first of the four
contiguous lease periods on auction is for. The second is the block number when the
auction will "begin to end", i.e. the first block of the Ending Period of the auction.

items.name: ReservedAmounts
docs: Amounts currently reserved in the accounts of the bidders currently winning
(sub-)ranges.

items.name: Winning
docs: The winning bids for each of the 10 ranges at each sample in the final Ending Period of
the current auction. The map's key is the 0-based index into the Sample Size. The
first sample of the ending period is 0; the last is `Sample Size - 1`.

constants.name: EndingPeriod
docs: The number of blocks over which an auction may be retroactively ended.

constants.name: SampleLength
docs: The length of each sample to take during the ending period.
`EndingPeriod` / `SampleLength` = Total # of Samples

items.name: Funds
docs: Info on all of the funds.

items.name: NewRaise
docs: The funds that have had additional contributions during the last block. This is used
in order to determine which funds should submit new or updated bids.

items.name: EndingsCount
docs: The number of auctions that have entered into their ending period so far.

items.name: NextFundIndex
docs: Tracker for the next available fund index

constants.name: PalletId
docs: `PalletId` for the crowdloan pallet. An appropriate value could be
`PalletId(*b"py/cfund")`

constants.name: MinContribution
docs: The minimum amount that may be contributed into a crowdloan. Should almost certainly be
at least `ExistentialDeposit`.

constants.name: RemoveKeysLimit
docs: Max number of storage keys to remove per extrinsic call.

constants.name: BrokerId
docs: The ParaId of the coretime chain.

constants.name: BrokerPotLocation
docs: The coretime chain pot location.

items.name: Cursor
docs: The currently active migration to run and its cursor.
`None` indicates that no migration is running.

items.name: Historic
docs: Set of all successfully executed migrations.
This is used as blacklist, to not re-execute migrations that have not been removed from the
codebase yet. Governance can regularly clear this out via `clear_historic`.

constants.name: CursorMaxLen
docs: The maximal length of an encoded cursor.
A good default needs to selected such that no migration will ever have a cursor with MEL
above this limit. This is statically checked in `integrity_test`.

constants.name: IdentifierMaxLen
docs: The maximal length of an encoded identifier.
A good default needs to selected such that no migration will ever have an identifier
with MEL above this limit. This is statically checked in `integrity_test`.

items.name: QueryCounter
docs: The latest available query index.

items.name: Queries
docs: The ongoing queries.

items.name: AssetTraps
docs: The existing asset traps.
Key is the blake2 256 hash of (origin, versioned `Assets`) pair. Value is the number of
times this pair has been trapped (usually just 1 if it exists at all).

items.name: SafeXcmVersion
docs: Default version to encode XCM when latest version of destination is unknown. If `None`,
then the destinations whose XCM version is unknown are considered unreachable.

items.name: SupportedVersion
docs: The Latest versions that we know various locations support.

items.name: VersionNotifiers
docs: All locations that we have requested version notifications from.

items.name: VersionNotifyTargets
docs: The target locations that are subscribed to our version changes, as well as the most recent
of our versions we informed them of.

items.name: VersionDiscoveryQueue
docs: Destinations whose latest XCM version we would like to know. Duplicates not allowed, and
the `u32` counter is the number of times that a send to the destination has been attempted,
which is used as a prioritization.

items.name: CurrentMigration
docs: The current migration's stage, if any.

items.name: RemoteLockedFungibles
docs: Fungible assets which we know are locked on a remote chain.

items.name: LockedFungibles
docs: Fungible assets which we know are locked on this chain.

items.name: XcmExecutionSuspended
docs: Global suspension state of the XCM executor.

items.name: ShouldRecordXcm
docs: Whether or not incoming XCMs (both executed locally and received) should be recorded.
Only one XCM program will be recorded at a time.
This is meant to be used in runtime APIs, and it's advised it stays false
for all other use cases, so as to not degrade regular performance.
Only relevant if this pallet is being used as the [`xcm_executor::traits::RecordXcm`]
implementation in the XCM executor configuration.

items.name: RecordedXcm
docs: If [`ShouldRecordXcm`] is set to true, then the last XCM program executed locally
will be stored here.
Runtime APIs can fetch the XCM that was executed by accessing this value.
Only relevant if this pallet is being used as the [`xcm_executor::traits::RecordXcm`]
implementation in the XCM executor configuration.

items.name: AuthorizedAliases
docs: Map of authorized aliasers of local origins. Each local location can authorize a list of
other locations to alias into it. Each aliaser is only valid until its inner `expiry`
block number.

value.note: This extrinsic uses `WeightLimit::Unlimited`, please migrate to `limited_teleport_assets` or `transfer_assets`
This extrinsic uses `WeightLimit::Unlimited`, please migrate to `limited_reserve_transfer_assets` or `transfer_assets`

constants.name: AdvertisedXcmVersion
docs: The latest supported version that we advertise. Generally just set it to
`pallet_xcm::CurrentXcmVersion`.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: The runtime `Call` type.

associatedTypes.name: RemoteLockConsumerIdentifier
docs: The ID type for local consumers of remote locks.

items.name: Authorities
docs: The current authorities set

items.name: ValidatorSetId
docs: The current validator set id

items.name: NextAuthorities
docs: Authorities set scheduled to be used with the next session

items.name: SetIdSession
docs: A mapping from BEEFY set ID to the index of the *most recent* session for which its
members were responsible.
This is only used for validating equivocation proofs. An equivocation proof must
contains a key-ownership proof for a given session, therefore we need a way to tie
together sessions and BEEFY set ids, i.e. we need to validate that a validator
was the owner of a given key on a given session, and what the active set ID was
during that session.
TWOX-NOTE: `ValidatorSetId` is not under user control.

items.name: GenesisBlock
docs: Block number where BEEFY consensus is enabled/started.
By changing this (through privileged `set_new_genesis()`), BEEFY consensus is effectively
restarted from the newly set block number.

constants.name: MaxAuthorities
docs: The maximum number of authorities that can be added.

constants.name: MaxNominators
docs: The maximum number of nominators for each validator.

constants.name: MaxSetIdSessionEntries
docs: The maximum number of entries to keep in the set id to session index mapping.
Since the `SetIdSession` map is only used for validating equivocations this
value should relate to the bonding duration of whatever staking system is
being used (if any). If equivocation handling is not enabled then this value
can be zero.

associatedTypes.name: BeefyId
docs: Authority identifier type

associatedTypes.name: KeyOwnerProof
docs: The proof of key ownership, used for validating equivocation reports
The proof must include the session index and validator count of the
session at which the equivocation occurred.

items.name: RootHash
docs: Latest MMR Root hash.

items.name: NumberOfLeaves
docs: Current size of the MMR (number of leaves).

items.name: Nodes
docs: Hashes of the nodes in the MMR.
Note this collection only contains MMR peaks, the inner nodes (and leaves)
are pruned and only stored in the Offchain DB.

items.name: BeefyAuthorities
docs: Details of current BEEFY authority set.

items.name: BeefyNextAuthorities
docs: Details of next BEEFY authority set.
This storage entry is used as cache for calls to `update_beefy_next_authority_set`.

items.name: PermanentSlots
docs: Assigned permanent slots, with their start lease period, and duration.

items.name: PermanentSlotCount
docs: Number of assigned (and active) permanent slots.

items.name: TemporarySlots
docs: Assigned temporary slots.

items.name: TemporarySlotCount
docs: Number of assigned temporary slots.

items.name: ActiveTemporarySlotCount
docs: Number of active temporary slots in current slot lease period.

items.name: MaxTemporarySlots
docs: The max number of temporary slots that can be assigned.

items.name: MaxPermanentSlots
docs: The max number of permanent slots that can be assigned.

constants.name: PermanentSlotLeasePeriodLength
docs: The number of lease periods a permanent parachain slot lasts.

constants.name: TemporarySlotLeasePeriodLength
docs: The number of lease periods a temporary parachain slot lasts.

constants.name: MaxTemporarySlotPerLeasePeriod
docs: The max number of temporary slots to be scheduled per lease periods.

items.name: ValidatorsToRetire
docs: Validators that should be retired, because their Parachain was deregistered.

items.name: ValidatorsToAdd
docs: Validators that should be added.

items.name: MigrationProcess
docs: Migration progress.
This stores the snapshot of the last migrated keys. It can be set into motion and move
forward by any of the means provided by this pallet.

items.name: AutoLimits
docs: The limits that are imposed on automatic migrations.
If set to None, then no automatic migration happens.

items.name: SignedMigrationMaxLimits
docs: The maximum limits that the signed migration could use.
If not set, no signed submission is allowed.

constants.name: MaxKeyLen
docs: Maximal number of bytes that a key can have.
FRAME itself does not limit the key length.
The concrete value must therefore depend on your storage usage.
A [`frame_support::storage::StorageNMap`] for example can have an arbitrary number of
keys which are then hashed and concatenated, resulting in arbitrarily long keys.
Use the *state migration RPC* to retrieve the length of the longest key in your
storage: <https://github.com/paritytech/substrate/issues/11642>
The migration will halt with a `Halted` event if this value is too small.
Since there is no real penalty from over-estimating, it is advised to use a large
value. The default is 512 byte.
Some key lengths for reference:
- [`frame_support::storage::StorageValue`]: 32 byte
- [`frame_support::storage::StorageMap`]: 64 byte
- [`frame_support::storage::StorageDoubleMap`]: 96 byte
For more info see
<https://www.shawntabrizi.com/blog/substrate/querying-substrate-storage-via-rpc/>

items.name: Key
docs: The `AccountId` of the sudo key.

associatedTypes.name: RuntimeCall
docs: A sudo-able call.

apis.name: Core

methods.name: version
docs: Returns the version of the runtime.

methods.name: execute_block
docs: Execute the given block.

methods.name: initialize_block
docs: Initialize a block with the given header and return the runtime executive mode.
The `Core` runtime api that every Substrate runtime needs to implement.

apis.name: XcmPaymentApi

methods.name: query_acceptable_payment_assets
docs: Returns a list of acceptable payment assets.
# Arguments
* `xcm_version`: Version.

methods.name: query_xcm_weight
docs: Returns a weight needed to execute a XCM.
# Arguments
* `message`: `VersionedXcm`.

methods.name: query_weight_to_asset_fee
docs: Converts a weight into a fee for the specified `AssetId`.
# Arguments
* `weight`: convertible `Weight`.
* `asset`: `VersionedAssetId`.

methods.name: query_delivery_fees
docs: Get delivery fees for sending a specific `message` to a `destination`.
These always come in a specific asset, defined by the chain.
# Arguments
* `message`: The message that'll be sent, necessary because most delivery fees are based on the
size of the message.
* `destination`: The destination to send the message to. Different destinations may use
different senders that charge different fees.
A trait of XCM payment API.
API provides functionality for obtaining:
* the weight required to execute an XCM message,
* a list of acceptable `AssetId`s for message execution payment,
* the cost of the weight in the specified acceptable `AssetId`.
* the fees for an XCM message delivery.
To determine the execution weight of the calls required for
[`xcm::latest::Instruction::Transact`] instruction, `TransactionPaymentCallApi` can be used.

apis.name: DryRunApi

methods.name: dry_run_call
docs: Dry run call V2.

methods.name: dry_run_xcm
docs: Dry run XCM program
API for dry-running extrinsics and XCM programs to get the programs that need to be passed to the fees API.
All calls return a vector of tuples (location, xcm) where each "xcm" is executed in "location".
If there's local execution, the location will be "Here".
This vector can be used to calculate both execution and delivery fees.
Calls or XCMs might fail when executed, this doesn't mean the result of these calls will be an `Err`.
In those cases, there might still be a valid result, with the execution error inside it.
The only reasons why these calls might return an error are listed in the [`Error`] enum.

methods.name: submit_report_double_voting_unsigned_extrinsic
docs: Submits an unsigned extrinsic to report a double voting equivocation. The caller
must provide the double voting proof and a key ownership proof
(should be obtained using `generate_key_ownership_proof`). The
extrinsic will be unsigned and should only be accepted for local
authorship (not to be broadcast to the network). This method returns
`None` when creation of the extrinsic fails, e.g. if equivocation
reporting is disabled for the given runtime (i.e. this method is
hardcoded to return `None`). Only useful in an offchain context.

methods.name: submit_report_fork_voting_unsigned_extrinsic
docs: Submits an unsigned extrinsic to report a fork voting equivocation. The caller
must provide the fork voting proof (the ancestry proof should be obtained using
`generate_ancestry_proof`) and a key ownership proof (should be obtained using
`generate_key_ownership_proof`). The extrinsic will be unsigned and should only
be accepted for local authorship (not to be broadcast to the network). This method
returns `None` when creation of the extrinsic fails, e.g. if equivocation
reporting is disabled for the given runtime (i.e. this method is
hardcoded to return `None`). Only useful in an offchain context.

methods.name: submit_report_future_block_voting_unsigned_extrinsic
docs: Submits an unsigned extrinsic to report a future block voting equivocation. The caller
must provide the future block voting proof and a key ownership proof
(should be obtained using `generate_key_ownership_proof`).
The extrinsic will be unsigned and should only be accepted for local
authorship (not to be broadcast to the network). This method returns
`None` when creation of the extrinsic fails, e.g. if equivocation
reporting is disabled for the given runtime (i.e. this method is
hardcoded to return `None`). Only useful in an offchain context.

methods.name: generate_key_ownership_proof
docs: Generates a proof of key ownership for the given authority in the
given set. An example usage of this module is coupled with the
session historical module to prove that a given authority key is
tied to a given staking identity during a specific session. Proofs
of key ownership are necessary for submitting equivocation reports.
NOTE: even though the API takes a `set_id` as parameter the current
implementations ignores this parameter and instead relies on this
method being called at the correct block height, i.e. any point at
which the given set id is live on-chain. Future implementations will
instead use indexed data through an offchain worker, not requiring
older states to be available.

methods.name: generate_ancestry_proof
docs: Generates a proof that the `prev_block_number` is part of the canonical chain at
`best_known_block_number`.
API necessary for BEEFY voters.

When called at block B, it will return the set of authorities that should be

methods.name: submit_report_equivocation_unsigned_extrinsic
docs: Submits an unsigned extrinsic to report an equivocation. The caller
must provide the equivocation proof and a key ownership proof
(should be obtained using `generate_key_ownership_proof`). The
extrinsic will be unsigned and should only be accepted for local
authorship (not to be broadcast to the network). This method returns
`None` when creation of the extrinsic fails, e.g. if equivocation
reporting is disabled for the given runtime (i.e. this method is
hardcoded to return `None`). Only useful in an offchain context.

methods.name: generate_key_ownership_proof
docs: Generates a proof of key ownership for the given authority in the
given set. An example usage of this module is coupled with the
session historical module to prove that a given authority key is
tied to a given staking identity during a specific session. Proofs
of key ownership are necessary for submitting equivocation reports.
NOTE: even though the API takes a `set_id` as parameter the current
implementations ignore this parameter and instead rely on this
method being called at the correct block height, i.e. any point at
which the given set id is live on-chain. Future implementations will
instead use indexed data through an offchain worker, not requiring
older states to be available.

methods.name: current_set_id
docs: Get current GRANDPA authority set id.
APIs for integrating the GRANDPA finality gadget into runtimes.
This should be implemented on the runtime side.
This is primarily used for negotiating authority-set changes for the
gadget. GRANDPA uses a signaling model of changing authority sets:
changes should be signaled with a delay of N blocks, and then automatically
applied in the runtime after those N blocks have passed.
The consensus protocol will coordinate the handoff externally.

apis.name: GenesisBuilder

methods.name: build_state
docs: Build `RuntimeGenesisConfig` from a JSON blob not using any defaults and store it in the
storage.
In the case of a FRAME-based runtime, this function deserializes the full
`RuntimeGenesisConfig` from the given JSON blob and puts it into the storage. If the
provided JSON blob is incorrect or incomplete or the deserialization fails, an error
is returned.
Please note that provided JSON blob must contain all `RuntimeGenesisConfig` fields, no
defaults will be used.

methods.name: get_preset
docs: Returns a JSON blob representation of the built-in `RuntimeGenesisConfig` identified by
`id`.
If `id` is `None` the function should return JSON blob representation of the default
`RuntimeGenesisConfig` struct of the runtime. Implementation must provide default
`RuntimeGenesisConfig`.
Otherwise function returns a JSON representation of the built-in, named
`RuntimeGenesisConfig` preset identified by `id`, or `None` if such preset does not
exist. Returned `Vec<u8>` contains bytes of JSON blob (patch) which comprises a list of
(potentially nested) key-value pairs that are intended for customizing the default
runtime genesis config. The patch shall be merged (rfc7386) with the JSON representation
of the default `RuntimeGenesisConfig` to create a comprehensive genesis config that can
be used in `build_state` method.

methods.name: preset_names
docs: Returns a list of identifiers for available builtin `RuntimeGenesisConfig` presets.
The presets from the list can be queried with [`GenesisBuilder::get_preset`] method. If
no named presets are provided by the runtime the list is empty.
API to interact with `RuntimeGenesisConfig` for the runtime
